🦫 Beaver's Bathroom Blitz is LIVE! 🚽 

So there I was driving back from my honeymoon in Florida, and we hit THREE Buc-ee's in one day (don't judge). And it hit me - every single time, those bathrooms were SPOTLESS. Sparkling clean, no matter how busy.

I started watching the attendants work. These people are absolute machines. Customers streaming in non-stop, and they're plunging, mopping, restocking like their life depends on it. I thought... this would make an incredible game.

So I built it.

Beaver's Bathroom Blitz puts YOU in the shoes of a bathroom attendant. Think you can handle the chaos?

🪠 Plunge, scrub, mop & restock through 6 increasingly wild shifts
👑 Handle VIP customers (no pressure, they tip double!)
🔍 Survive surprise Health Inspector visits
⚡ Use power-ups when the tour buses roll in
🏆 Climb from "Potty Trainee" to the legendary "Golden Plunger"

Next time you stop at Buc-ee's, tip your bathroom attendant. They're the real MVPs.

Free to play - unlock the full experience for $2.99!

Big news.  This happened last night.  EEEK! 

I met @__michelle.elizabeth__ over 3 years ago on a school trip to Disney World. 

Over those three years, I got to know her and her kids really well, first inviting them to attend my church and then to join my small group.  I got to know her as a friend first and was so impressed with how she loved and served others. 

Then back in May, I asked her out on a date and it’s been wonderful getting to know her even better. Last night, I asked her a new question.  And she said yes! 

Here’s a cool thing - looking back over my pictures, the first time we were ever near each other was at a performance of Into the Woods at the Sugar Hill bowl 4 and a half years ago.  I didn’t know it but I sat right in front of her! (See the pic!) But I wouldn’t meet her for another year.  I asked her to marry me in that very spot.  We had to recreate the photo of the first time we were ever in each other’s presence. 

I’m so beyond blessed to have found an amazing woman who loves Jesus so much, and loves me so well.  And I’m so blessed to be joining her family and for her family to join mine.

I’m going to be stepdad to 3 amazing kids, Andrew, Bella, and Sara!  They are great friends with my nieces and nephews. (@andrew.c.trotter is a crazy talented pianist and wants to be a composer - how cool is that for this filmmaker?!) 

Also - it’s so special that I was able to give her my mom’s engagement ring. My mom would have been best friends with Michelle!

So excited for our life together!
Photo by Jeremiah Daws on November 24, 2025.

I did a thing.

You know that pit in your stomach when your 10-year-old asks for Apple Music?

You want to say yes. They deserve real music. But you’ve seen what’s out there. The album covers. The lyrics. The stuff that pops up when they search.

Apple Music’s parental controls are useless. The “clean” filter still lets through a ton of inappropriate content - album covers, themes, stuff you don’t want your kids exposed to.

So I built what I couldn’t find: SafeTunes.

Here’s how it works:

You approve albums. They play any songs from those albums. That’s it. No browsing. No “related artists.” No algorithms pushing garbage. Just the music you said yes to.

Hide the album covers you don’t want them seeing. Approve Taylor Swift’s music but hide that album cover? Done. One tap.

Get notified when they search for something inappropriate. They searched for what?! You’ll know immediately. No access, but you get the heads up for the conversation.

Bible verses instead of error messages. When content gets blocked, they see encouraging scripture (Philippians 4:8, Psalm 101:3) — not shame.

Works on ANY device they have. Chromebook from school? Old iPad? Your Android phone? Yes. All of it. No app store, no downloads.

They can request music, you approve from anywhere. “Mom, can I get the new Sabrina Carpenter album?” One tap. Approved or denied. Takes 5 seconds.

Setup takes 5 minutes. One family code. They pick their profile. They’re listening. You’re sleeping at night.

Want to help me test it?

Message me directly and I’ll send you a coupon code for FREE access for a full year. I just need honest feedback as you use it with your family.

Normally, it’s a 7-day free trial at $4.99/month after that. Cancel anytime.

I built this because I’m tired of choosing between “let them have garbage” or “make them listen to VeggieTales until college.”

If you’ve been wrestling with this exact problem, this is your answer.

www.getsafetunes.com

This week I’m leading Creative Tech at Artios Outpost, teaching 10-12 year olds how to 3D model. We started with Fusion360 and then jumped into ZBrush sculpting. It’s been awesome watching them create and pick up new skills so fast.

Finally put up the zipline from our pirate ship treehouse. Been meaning to do it for a while, and the boys took the lead and made it happen.

Everyone in the family had to try it. Some were braver than others. A few questionable landings. Lots of laughs.

I love this crew. We’re always up to something fun, and I wouldn’t trade it for anything.
#Zipline #PirateShipTreehouse #MemorialDayFun #familytime

I spent this past Saturday afternoon with my youngest niece, Claire!  We went to Starbucks and then got some fabric at Hobby Lobby and went to my workshop to make a stuffed animal. The bear is not my best work (she wanted a lion but it didn’t measure up so we made it a bear) but she loves it. She named it Ethan - her brother’s name is Ethan. She thought it would be funny to say, “where’s Ethan?” And then say to her brother, “not you, Ethan!” That brought her endless amounts of laughter. I’m thankful for special time with her. She’s a joy to be around.

I had the pleasure of being a small part of @artios_sugarhill ’s high school production of Little Women. In addition to documenting behind the scenes and filming the show with multiple cameras, I also was able to 3D model and 3D print some oil lamps that were mounted to the set walls.  My favorite part is the magnetic bottom to allow for access to the electronics! 

The show was fantastic!  I love being able to use my talents to add to the already amazing talents of everyone involved in the show.  Such a great community! 

#3dprinting #fusion360 #theaterkids
Been busy running parts through my @tormach 1100MX mill. Still working great. #machining #cncmachining

Soo excited to finally have my @saundersmachineworks fixture plate setup on my @tormach CNC mill. This thing is fantastic and makes my setup process soo much faster. Makes an already incredible machine even more incredible!

Treehouse update. We had a productive build day. Just about finished with the captain’s cabin. And we now have railing on the top level. Still have some details to add but at least it’s safe for the kiddos to play in all the way up. Love how this thing has turned out. Planning a camp out in the treehouse next week. Can’t wait!
Treehouse update. First day it wasn’t super hot outside so I built the ship’s ladder leading up to the top floor. Nice to make a little progress on this thing. It’s been a few months.

3D modeled and printed a replacement part for my computer keyboard. A few minutes of my time and a few pennies worth of plastic and good as new.  I’m going to be teaching this skill this fall to some middle schoolers. I’m excited!  #3dprinting #fusion360

Here’s another prop I made for a high school production of The Little Mermaid. King Triton’s staff. I 3D modeled it using a still from the animated movie so the profile is just about screen accurate.  Very happy with the look but huge lesson learned on this one - don’t 3D print stage props. This thing broke a lot and I ended up having to fix it with epoxy glue and clay multiple times - sometimes even during the performances!  That was stressful but fun to experience the excitement of live theater in some small way. #propmaking #stageprops #littlemermaid

I was asked by a local high school to make a metal frame so that Ursula could be pushed around on stage with her tentacles in tow. Fun project for a really impressive show. @artios_sugarhill #theaterproduction #welding

So my family has recently gotten into RPG gaming. It’s been a lot of fun to play and laugh and be creative together. I’ve also been building and 3D printing terrain and minis and painting them with my nephews.  Such a fun hobby.

I’ve been teaching my nephews how to build little robots using Arduino… which has required me to learn more Arduino. Fun stuff!  We’ve got big plans for these little guys but for now we have them motorized and remote controlled. Not bad.

Another treehouse update. Working on the back wall of the captain’s cabin with a bay window. Plus check out my nephew’s drawing of the ship as it was when it sailed the seven seas - before it was shipwrecked here in the trees of our backyard. 😁😁😁 So fun!  Slowly making progress.
Treehouse update. This is so much fun!  Walls and top floor well on their way!
Pirate ship themed treehouse is looking great!  How big is too big for a treehouse?!? #treehouse #treehouselife
Treehouse update!  Phase three has begun construction!  #treehouse
Treehouse update!  Phase one has bamboo railing!  We had to do a celebration dance led by my brother @jon_daws_1984!  Super happy with how this is turning out!  #treehouse #maker #bamboo
Quick update on the treehouse. Have railings up. Adding vertical bamboo to the railing this week with the kiddos. Almost done with phase one.

I designed these sign holders to better mark the rooms in the kids hallway at my church. @shadowbrook_church.  I started by designing and 3D modeling them in #fusion360 and then #3dprinted them. I love solving problems like this. #maker

I love to organize things. I spent time organizing my niece’s craft corner.  It had exploded all over the workout room. We worked together to get it under control. This was so much fun!  I love that she has her own “workshop” to let her creativity flourish. Just gotta keep it in check. #organization
Spent some time this week harvesting and heat treating bamboo that will be used in our treehouse.  I love working with non-traditional materials. Bamboo is so cool! #maker #bamboo #treehouse

Update on the family treehouse. We’re going with a pirate ship/Swiss Family treehouse aesthetic. 😁 This has been such a fun and challenging process. Starting to experiment with railing - I’m thinking bamboo!  Anyone have bamboo growing in their backyard that they don’t want?

Phase one of our family treehouse is complete. We have a finished flooring and a ship ladder to get up. Henry and his mom are doing their homeschool up there. That is so fun. I’m so grateful for this project. We have some huge plans. Excited to see it develop over the next several months.

Now selling 3 models of @fidgetcrafttoys - the DOMINO, the DICE, and the new COIN. The coin opens up new possibilities for wheels, eyes, etc… play testers have been loving them. Link to purchase in bio. #edc #fidgettoys #satisfying

Dreams came true today… for me and my nieces and nephews.  We finally made a huge leap forward on our treehouse.  I’ve always wanted to make a treehouse!  It’s so high. Next step is to do railings and a safe way up. Then comes the bells and whistles like crows nest and zip line. Haha.
Behind the scenes video - Machining a tiny adapter plate on my @tormach 1100 MX CNC mill. #madewithtormach This is the smallest part I’ve ever machined!

I borrowed this powerful drill from a friend. It’s fairly old and started tripping breakers as I would plug it in. I noticed the cord was looking bad. Took it apart and found a bunch of exposed wires inside. “So there’s your problem…” I bought a replacement cord and swapped it out. I love getting useful tools like this back into circulation.
I made a hotend adapter plate for a 3D printer today. This is the smallest item I have ever machined!  That was a lot of fun. #madewithtormach

I created magnetic shoes for Lego Minifigs that work with @fidgetcrafttoys!  Oh my goodness!  Game changer.

www.FidgetCraft.com

I gave my big brother a set of FidgetCraft tonight (the DICE and DOMINO) and he discovered this amazing trick you can do with it. My nephews and I lost it! So cool. I don’t understand it but I love it! #fidget #fidgettoys #fidgettoy #fidgetslider #stemtoys #stemtoysforkids

Working on the injection mold for my @fidgetcrafttoys - once I get the mold working, I’ll be able to crank out these toys faster and they’ll look like real things and not just 3D printed. #fidgettoys #injectionmolding #cncmachining

I was asked to fix a part on a golf cart that had been driven into a tree. I cut off the old bent part, machined a replacement, and welded it onto the original. A quick coat of paint and good as new. Let me know if I can fix anything for you! (Link in bio to my website to learn more) #recycle #welding #machining

Plus I’ve had loads of fun playing with it. It’s so satisfying!  Check it out over on my store. Link in bio!  #maker #edc #fidget #fidgettoys #satisfying #satisfyingvideos #haptic #asmr
Here’s my year end recap of some of the projects I worked on this year. #maker #makersgonnamake #machinist #madewithtormach

Someone asked me to make a replacement part for a German Mauser bayonet from WWII. I love having the right tools for the job!  I’m definitely putting my @tormach machines to good use. Check out my process! #cnc #cncmachining #cncmachinist #madewithtormach

This was a surprisingly fun project. I was asked to fix a trailer. The tongue had been bent pretty badly when it was jackknifed.  I cut off the bent portion and welded a new section on and reinforced the joint on the inside of the c channel. Then I relocated the spare tire assembly.  I love how it turned out!

I’ve said it before - everyone should learn how to weld. It’s such a handy thing to know.  Plus it’ll be useful in the coming zombie apocalypse.  Still waiting on someone to ask me to put spikes on their vehicle. One day. 😜 #welding #maker #diy #weldlife

Spent the afternoon with my nephews helping them sew Newsies bags for their group Halloween costume. I love that they’re already accomplished crafters that just needed a little assistance. Hudson sewed almost all of it and did a great job.  #newsies #halloweencostume #diy #sewing #sewingproject

I made this folding wall divider thing for the kids area at my church. It is meant to protect the sound and video booth wires from little hands. This project fought me from the beginning.  Just didn’t go smoothly at all. Guess that’s how it goes sometimes. I’m pleased with how it turned out but so very glad to be done with this one. #diy #maker

I was given this part to make 3 copies. It attaches to a film dolly. I modeled it in Fusion360 and made it in my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill. Really excited about how these turned out. Just gotta deburr and press in the pin and then I’ll take em to set and drop them off to the client. #gripgear #filmgear #movieset #cncmachining #madewithtormach
Electronic Tool Setter FTW!  Makes setup a breeze! #cncmachining #madewithtormach

Prepping some aluminum blocks for a part I’m making later this week. My new CNC machine made quick work of this. Just did a quick conversational program (point and click) and let it go. I was able to do other stuff around the shop while it worked. #efficiency #madewithtormach #cncmachining
Trying (and failing) to make a treehouse. Some projects feel doomed from the start. #maker #treehouse #diy
First parts made on my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill.  These are for a grip working in the film biz here in Atlanta. #cncmachining #gripgear #filmgear

I love being able to solve a problem that has caused a tremendous amount of frustration over the years. My family loves doing a backyard campfire with hotdogs and s’mores. But we always end up dropping the skewers into the dirt. And there is the risk of sitting or stepping on the hot metal forks. I finally solved these issues with a pretty simple design that works really well.  This was really fun to dream up and implement in a short amount of time. I love knowing how to weld. I highly recommend taking a class. #maker #fabrication #satisfyingvideos #satisfying #welding #smores #campfire

First project on my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill — Gussets for this 90 degree camera plate. Simple project but it made this infinitely faster than having to spot drill, drill, and tap 8 holes on the manual mill. Feeling more familiar with this amazing machine. #cncmachining #madewithtormach

Today was a long day. I took delivery of a new @tormach 1100MX CNC mill.  I’ve only been machining for about 21 months - I can’t believe I now have a manual mill and lathe AND a CNC mill and lathe. All glory belongs to God as HE is the provider of it all. I can’t wait to fire this baby up and start making cool stuff. This has been a crazy adventure. I’m humbled and soo grateful to be on this journey. More to come!  #madewithtormach #tormach #cncmachining #cncmilling #cncmill #cncmachinist

My nephews asked me to mod their arrows to be archery  tag arrows. I designed new arrow heads and nocks in Fusion360 and used my @tormach lathe to machine them. They turned out great and the boys love them. #cncmachining #madewithtormach #cnclathe #archery #archerytag #makersgonnamake #archerylife

I had the immense pleasure of fabricating this steadi-cam sled dolly for a production shooting in the Atlanta area. On top of that, I was able to visit the set to deliver it to the grip team working on the show. After I dropped it off, I got a text from the best boy telling me that the dolly was already used to film a shot!  Wow. How Marvelous! 😉🤫 I always wanted to make movies. I never dreamed I’d make gear that would be used to make movies. Life can be weird sometimes.  #maker #fabricator #productionlife #grip #gear #setlife

I love simple shop improvements. I welded this broom rack today. These brooms have been shuffled and stowed all over the place. This is so satisfying to finally have a spot for them. #welding #maker #shopupgrade
Photo by Jeremiah Daws on August 16, 2022.
Photo by Jeremiah Daws on August 07, 2022.
I’m starting a new project this week. This is going to be fun!!! #fabrication #gripgear #productiongear #setlife🎥

Spent the afternoon making homemade archery tag arrows with my nephew. The plastic tip was machined with my @Tormach lathe and the foam was cut out on a laser. I love quick projects like this… especially when they actually work!  #madewithtormach #machining #cncmachining

I was asked to 3D print a set of planetary gears for the Diesel instructor @lanier_tech. He wanted a handheld way to teach his students about planetary gears - (part of why big trucks are able to create so much power). I 3D printed the gears but the base kept failing for various reasons. I decided it would be much cooler (and faster) to machine the base out of aluminum. This turned out pretty cool. Fun to mix mediums like this.  #3dprinting #machining #cncmachining #cncmachinist

I taught my nephews how basic circuits work and they designed this box using batteries, switches, a motor, LEDs, and potentiometers. They even soldered some of the connections themselves.  I gotta admit, I want one for myself! There’s just something satisfying about a switch. Especially these cool red doomsday switches. 😂 #basicelectronics #circuits #simplecircuits #satisfyingvideos

Here’s another fun piece that’s part of a larger project. Made entirely on a Bridgeport Mill. #satisfyingvideos #manualmachinist #bridgeportmill #machinistsofinstagram #machiningvideooftheweek #machining

Last week I competed in the national @skillsusa competition in Atlanta. It’s a competition for students in trade school. My category was CNC Turning Specialist. I had to program a part from a blueprint and take some tests. I ended up placing 2nd in the nation. I got a silver medal and $3000!  There was a whole awards ceremony at State Farm Arena (see video). With my time in trade school coming to an end in a few weeks, this was an amazing finale to the whole experience. #cncmachining #tradeschool

Making the base to a bigger project. Will post final after I post all the parts. Each part was so much fun to make that I want to share them individually first. #manualmachinist #manualmachining #bridgeportmill #machining

Made this replacement part for a Diesel engine. (Not quite sure what it does.) Was given the broken part and had to 3D model it in Fusion360 and then program the tool paths. Then I ran the part in a CNC lathe. I’m still amazed that I know how to do this!  #cncmachinist #cncmachining #cnclathe

The motorsports team @lanier_tech asked me to make drive pins for one or their race cars. From what I can tell, these pins are part of what allows a race car tire to be quickly changed out. These are made out of 17-4 stainless steel which was very tricky to machine. A fun and challenging project. #machinist #maker #machining #cncmachining

Today I shipped off my first Etsy order! I’m enjoying dipping my toe into the product world. Here’s a fun little reel showing a little bit of the process of how I make my magnetic action figure blister packs. (Etsy store link in bio) #actionfigures #toys #makersgonnamake #vacuumforming

My latest creation - a magnetic action figure display pack.  Display your favorite action figures ANYWHERE!  I’m super excited about this - I’ve launched it for SALE on my Etsy store! (Link in BIO)

These magnetic action figure blister packs are perfect for the action figure collector who likes to open their toys but also display them.  The blister pack evokes the pristine nature of a action figures and the unique use of magnets allows you to display your action figures on refrigerators, filing cabinets, dry erase boards, etc...

These are the perfect size for Star Wars, G.I. Joe, or whatever 3 3/4 inch action figures you want to show off.
 
#actionfigures #toys #nostalgia #80stoys #display #custom #displaycase #blisterpackaging #vacuumforming #maker #etsy #etsyshop

I made replacement table locks for several mills around the shop at school. I used a CNC lathe and mill to make them.  Then I used tool black to treat the parts. They look pretty sweet and should resist rust for a long time. #machinist #machining #instamachinist

I made this lightsaber for a friend who wanted to cosplay Mitaka from Star Wars Visions for @starwarscelebration this weekend. I couldn’t attend this year but glad to be a small part of it.  Looks like everyone is having a blast. (Made on my CNC lathe @tormach, and my manual mill and lathe.). #starwarscelebration #lightsaber #machining #madewithtormach #machinist #cncmachining

One hour project - I made this skateboard rack out of scrap wood to help organize the garage. Took less than an hour. I’m so pleased with how this turned out.  Anyone in the Metro Atlanta area want one?  DM me. #woodworking #skateboardrack #garageorganization #organization

Little preview of a fun project for a friend. Star Wars related. He sent me an illustrator file and an hour later I had a part in my hand. Thanks to my trusty @tormach CNC lathe and of course the last year and a half of machinist training. Ha. Feels so good to have gotten more comfortable with my skills and the machines I’m blessed to own. (Little blemish from the chuck - will fix that.) Will post pics and vids of the rest of the project. This stuff keeps me up late at night - with excitement. #madewithtormach #machining #cncmachining #tormach
Photo by Jeremiah Daws in Lanier Technical College.
Photo by Jeremiah Daws on May 05, 2022.

Sometimes inspiration hits you late at night. Last night around midnight I decided to try and make a vacuum forming machine. So using a laser to cut out the box and an aluminum welder to make the frame, I was able to do a couple of successful vacuum pulls. This can be used to make molds or to create packaging for a future product. I am really excited about possibilities. I still have a lot to learn but this is a great first step.  #vacuumforming #maker

I’ve needed to build this shelf for a year. Not having a place for all my hand power tools was just driving me crazy. They were here and there and  kept getting shuffled around. Finally got to a point where it was affecting my productivity.  So here’s my Saturday morning project. So satisfying. #organized #saturdayproject

To quote Michael Corleone - “Just when I thought I was out, they pull me back in.” I guess the filmmaker part of me never really died. It was just dormant. I added this used camera rig to my gear list. It was time to upgrade my production quality for my YouTube channel and it opens up new opportunities. This exact camera was used for my old Disney productions. It shoots beautiful footage. Had to test it out on my nephew and nieces.  Can’t wait to shoot some machining/maker footage with it.
Photo by Jeremiah Daws on April 28, 2022.

Final project in my CNC mill class @lanier_tech - a turner’s cube. Created the GCode by hand. Ran the first side slow and then pushed the machine to it’s max speed for the other 5 sides. Kinda scary but amazing to see the speed and power of these machines. This project and class feels like the culmination of the last 16 months of study. Couldn’t be happier with my decision to go to trade school.  One more class this summer and I’m done!  #cncmill #cncmachining #cncmachinist #cncmachined #tradeschool

Cutting rack and pinion gears for a bigger project. It’s a cool process. Had a rough day @lanier_tech the other day. Crashed a Haas CNC lathe. I was running a manually programmed part and forgot to change the feed rate from threading (which is super fast) to parting (which should be super slow). I’ve been meaning to upgrade my miter saw stand for the last few years.  With the extra time during quarantine, I’ve been checking things off my list and the time has come.

I made this bench last year after I discovered @amazuluinc - they’re a local business that supplies bamboo and other tiki related material to the theme parks. I was so excited to walk their warehouse and see materials I’ve seen in Adventureland. Bamboo, thatched roofing material, eucalyptus wood... it made my theme park loving heart so happy.

This was my first time building with round material so I really had to rethink how I designed. The easiest way to wrap my brain around it was to build it out of straws and hot glue first - this saved me making costly mistakes when cutting the bamboo. I’m not in love with the final result but it was a really cool experience and I hope to do something else with bamboo sometime in the future.  #maker #bamboo

I now have a hidden room in my home!!! When I moved from California to the affordability and space of Florida, I knew I wanted to do something special with my home - something different than the traditional.  Well, I think I’ve accomplished it.  What kid doesn’t dream of having a hidden room?  I’m blessed to have the space, the resources, and now the abilities to make this dream a reality.

This was the longest, most challenging (and sometimes frustrating) building project I’ve ever undertaken.  I took it slow.  I took 2 steps forward and 1 step back.  But I learned so much through each and every dead end.

I started on this project back in late April of this year.  It was 5 months of frustrating and challenging work through late nights and on weekends.  Every few weeks, I was tempted to rip it down and forget I had ever attempted it.  But now that it’s done, I’m so glad I decided to move forward.

The space used to be my dining room - now I use it for VR and as an extra bedroom for guests.  I’m still working on the door lock mechanism and of course decorating all the shelves… SO MANY SHELVES!  Stay tuned for updates on those elements.

I know, I know, what’s the point of having a hidden room if you announce it on Social Media?!?! Well, it’s just too cool not to share, right?  I hope this inspires others to dive in and attempt something big.  Best advice I got through this process - if it’s something you don’t know how to do, take it SLOW.

#maker #hiddenroom #scoobydoo #webster #clue #batcave #templeofdoom #johnwick #harrypotter

I leveled up my welding game. I started out stick welding and it was just so darn difficult. I was getting frustrated and it made me not want to weld. Ahsoka Tano’s lightsabers from Star Wars: Clone Wars 3D printed for my friend, Macey. Trying to help her cosplay for an upcoming convention. These were fun and challenging. I 3D printed with two kinds of printers. The bigger pieces were printed using PLA and a typical extruded plastic printer. The smaller pieces were printed with liquid resin. Then I coated it with sandable filler/primer. Then LOTS of sanding. And then of course the paint job.  My first time trying to replicate chrome. Turned out alright. Room for improvement but not bad for a first try.  Thanks to @chinbeard for all his great videos on this stuff. #ahsokatano #lightsaber #maker #3dprinting #anycubicphoton #starwars #punishedprops

Major update to my Pirates themed office: My pirate skeleton is now an animatronic of sorts. Before, he was just a bit of decoration. But now- He’s got a motion sensor connected to a servo just above his neck that turns his head and lights up his eye whenever anyone walks by. I’ve been learning the basics of electronics and programming Arduino since Christmas. Finally got this guy working. Thanks to Philip Scott for helping me work through the neck/servo pivot point and to my Dad, my brother, and brother in law for their input as well. This was a fun one to work on. It’s simple but effective. Just enough subtle movement to really creep out the passerby. I’ve gotten some fun reactions from coworkers. I had never really built, crafted, programmed anything before a few years ago. I’m extremely grateful for all the information one can learn on YouTube. And I encourage anyone with an interest to learn something new to just dive in and do it. Can’t tell you how rewarding this journey has been over the last few years. Yeah, it’s a little silly. But I did this thing.  And I’m really proud of it. #maker #pirates #animatronic #arduino #programming #disney #skeleton #piratesofthecarribean #solideogloria

I made a Mickey ice cream bar!  We have a replica we use for video productions at Walt Disney World (so it doesn’t melt) but it’s oversized and it has the wrong stick. And it’s only useful for one angle. I wanted to make a true accurate replica. So here it is. I first cut the pattern out of insulation foam. Sanded and shaped it. Then covered it with a thermoplastic called worbla. Then cut the stick out  on a bandsaw. And finally a coat of brown airbrush paint and there ya have it. A very durable prop. What do you think?  Can it pass for the real thing?  #maker #worbla #foam #airbrush #waltdisneyworld #mickeyicecreambar #disneysnacks

Epic fail!  Sometimes you work on a project all day and it just doesn’t work. I prefer to learn by diving in and doing something. Yesterday’s project was to try molding and casting a 2 part mold with silicone and resin. I figured my newly 3D printed Ahsoka Tano lightsabers would be a good first project. Not a good idea. Should have done something simpler. Failed on every level. The mold was bad. The cast was had. And on top of it all, I mistakenly bought a flexible resin. Haha. Oh well. Kudos to everyone involved!  #maker #kalechurros #churros #waltdisneyworld #snacks #makersgonnamake #props #artdepartment #producer #manyhats #blessed

My first commissioned project!  I built this coat rack/shelf for my sister-in-law’s mother. My bro @jon.daws helped with it. I hope Beverly likes it!  #maker #woodworking #makestuff #makersgottamake #coatrack
Installed my new desk top at work. I love it! Goes really well with my faux wooden door. Slowly transforming my workspace into a Pirates themed dungeon. #maker #piratesofthecaribbean

This past week, I was told to move into a new office at work. I inherited a desk with lots of Mickey Mouse clippings decoupaged to the top. Not exactly my style. Rather than requesting a more typical desk, I decided to take it home and add my spin to it. (So nice to be back in my workshop.) One step closer to turning my office into Pirates of the Caribbean. Can’t wait to get it back into the office.  #maker #workshop #piratesofthecaribbean

Made this California Casual Patio Side Table.  First bit of homework from an online woodworking course I’m taking from @steveramsey_wwmm. Learned some helpful new techniques.  What color should I paint it? #maker #theweekendwoodworker #woodworking
A broomstick-inspired wand design. Made with a lathe and belt sander. #maker #harrypotter #etsystore?
I made a wizard wand with my drill press lathe. And I filmed some of the process. Check it out! #harrypotter #maker #lathe #nerd #woodworking

Experimenting with turning wood. Converted my drill press into a lathe. Always wanted to try this. It’s super fun but there’s a reason lathes are horizontal rather than vertical. (My hands are numb!) If I want to do this a lot, I’ll have  to get a traditional lathe. But until then, it’s fun to mess around with. I should make a wand from Harry Potter. But which one?  Hmm. #maker #lathe #turningwood #cantstopwontstop #makersgonnamake #whathaveyoumadetoday

I made a basic marionette with my nephew. Helping him with a school project. I want to try it again back at my workshop and improve the design. Fun first try. #maker #somestringsonme #puppet #marionette #pinocchio #lonelygoatherd #soundofmusic

This one takes some explaining. My favorite Indiana Jones movie is Temple of Doom. You can’t beat that bridge scene and Short Round rules!  I wanted to make a simple prop from that film. So I decided to make the Voodoo doll of Indiana Jones. (I do not advocate or promote the practice of Voodoo. I just think the prop is cool. 😜) I sculpted the head instead of carving it out of wood - which I kind of regret but I’m still pleased with how it turned out.  Fun to combine my basic sewing skills with my basic sculpting skills. #maker #novoodoo #shortround #indianajones #sewing #prop #movieprop #templeofdoom #tradersamseasteregg

Made this Star Wars: Clone Wars blaster for my coworker @dannyboy973. Pretty simple build. MDF, foam and pvc. Used techniques I learned from Punished Props (@chinbeard). Took a few liberties on the design and paint but I think it turned out okay. #maker #prop #starwars #captainrex #funtomakestuffforotherpeople

Latest project: Faux wood and iron door for my cubicle. Trying to slowly transition it into a themed workspace. This is step one. Amazing what you can do with foam pulled from a dumpster, a little paint, and a lot of free time. #maker #notwood #piratesofthecaribbean #ifpixarcandoitwhynotyellowshoes

Latest project - corner desk/work station for my workshop. I love it! I even tried my hand at some electrical work wiring a switch for some overhead lights. And added some French cleat storage. Pretty neat!  #maker #workshop #imhavingtoomuchfun

Sometimes the creative muse makes you do strange things. I think mine may be drunk. She told me to spend my entire weekend making a 1:12 scale miniature wooden crate. Why?  I may never know. But I’m in love with making miniatures now.  #singlenokids #maker #gohomemuseyouredrunk #whatshouldimakenext

Almost one year ago I decided to start building things. I was inspired by the many art department friends I’ve made over the last few years.  I figured if they can do it, maybe I could too. Many many YouTube tutorials later, I’ve learned how to sew, woodwork, sculpt and work with several different kinds of materials like thermoplastic and foam. It’s fun to look back over my Instagram. I built a Rocketeer helmet, a messenger bag, Maui’s fishhook, furniture, and various other bits of geeky goodness. I challenge anyone with a desire to do something creative to just go and do it - it’s extremely rewarding.  Here’s my last little project for 2017 - a wooden dock/bridge complete with weathering and fun little details. Can’t wait to see what 2018 brings!  #maker #ificandoitsocanyou #make2018great #makersgonnamake
Having way too much fun with my airbrush.  Painted a generic plastic hummer to look battle damaged and worn. Fun way to make cheap toys look expensive. #maker #newhobby
I spent the last few days modifying a Han Solo Nerf blaster to be more screen accurate. First time using my new airbrush that Santa brought. That was a lot of fun. #hanshotfirst #maker #singlenokids

I’ve been playing around with paper replicas I found from the RPF. (Replica Prop Forum.) Amazing the things you can make with a printer, paper, tea bag, water, and tape. Bonus points if you can name the movies these props are from. #ilovemovieprops
My weekend project. #dvdblurayshelf #maker #learningfromyoutubetutorials

My new hobby has become working with wood. I’ve turned my garage into a workshop. So far, I’ve only built workbenches and such for my shop but it’s been really fun to learn a new skill and I’m hoping to attempt some ambitious furniture projects soon. #maker
Made my baby niece Daisy a Rey costume and stuffed lightsaber.  I think she likes it. #starwars #babycosplay #sewingisfun
Attempted my first sand castle today. It's amazing what you can learn on YouTube. Sculpted this with a metal spatula and a pen. #makestuff #evenonvacation

Bat-arangs for the nephews. Cut the base form out of insulation foam on the band saw. Then covered it in black worbla (thermoplastic). Simple build but the kiddos love it and it's non-lethal. :) #batman #makethings

One of my objectives on my current Georgia vacation is to do some woodworking with my dad. He used to build stuff all the time while I was growing up but I never took an interest. Now with my new found interest in making, I'm catching up on all those missed opportunities. Yesterday, my dad and I built this folding workbench for his shop. I designed the entire thing in Sketchup. Huge sense of accomplishment. Best day ever building with my dad!
